w:r.w:t: Based on: Česnauskas M. Asmeninio sodo ar daržo informacinė sistema
Bachelor's Final Degree Project — Kaunas University of Technology, 2026

This document is a strict technical specification for a web-based Personal Garden/Allotment Information System. Every requirement, entity, use case, and business rule stated here is derived directly from the original BBP thesis. Implementations MUST conform exactly to this specification.

The system enables individual garden owners (or groups of owners) to plan, monitor, and manage personal garden or allotment activities. It replaces fragmented, manual processes (notes, separate apps, memory) with a unified, data-driven platform.
Interactive plot plan with visual zone & plant management
Multi-year planning history with crop rotation support
Plant condition tracking with status history
Integration with external weather forecast API (Meteo.lt)
Integration with external plant care API (Perenual)
Multi-user collaboration on a single plot (with role-based access)
MVC / Layered (UI Layer, Controller Layer, Data Model Layer)
Server-side PDF generation from plot data
Must work on desktop and mobile (different screen resolutions)
The system has three user roles:
Unauthenticated visitor. Can only access the registration and login pages.
The primary authenticated user. Can manage their own plots, plants, calendar, inventory, community features.
Has all garden owner capabilities plus the ability to view, search, and delete any user account.
NOTE: Garden Owner is a specialization (inheritance) of User. The system stores a role field on the User table ('owner' or 'admin').
The system is divided into three sub-domains for use cases:
All use cases are specified below. CRUD use cases that are straightforward are marked as such. Complex algorithmic use cases receive full flow descriptions.
Create a new user account in the system.
User is NOT authenticated in the system.
A new user account is created and the user is redirected to the login page. OR an error message is shown if registration fails.

1. Guest initiates registration. 2. System displays registration form. 3. Guest fills in required data. 4. System validates form completeness and correctness. If invalid → show error, allow correction (go to step 3). 5. System checks email uniqueness. If already registered → show error, allow correction (go to step 3). 6. System saves user data, creates account, shows success message. 7. System redirects user to login page.
Authenticate a registered user and grant access to the system.
User is authenticated and redirected to the main page. OR error message shown if login fails.

1. User initiates login. 2. System displays login form. 3. User may choose 'Forgot password' — if so, UC-04 is executed, then return here. 4. User enters credentials. 5. System validates credentials. If invalid → show error, allow retry. 6. System authenticates user, creates session, redirects to main page.
User session is destroyed; user is redirected to login page.
1. User initiates logout. 2. System destroys session data. 3. System shows logout success message. 4. System redirects to login page.
Allow a user to recover a forgotten password via email verification code.
Password is updated and user is shown the login form. OR error message shown.

1. User initiates forgot password. 2. System displays email input form. 3. User enters email. 4. System checks if email exists in DB. If not → error, allow retry. 5. System generates reset code, triggers email send via email server boundary. 6. Email server sends message to user. 7. User receives email with code. 8. System shows code input field. 9. User enters code. 10. System verifies code. If wrong → error, allow retry or regenerate code. 11. System shows new password form. 12. User enters new password. 13. System validates password meets requirements. If not → error, allow retry. 14. System updates password in DB, shows success, redirects to login.
UC-05: Edit Account Data (Redaguoti paskyros duomenis)
Allow an authenticated user to view and update their profile data.
Account data updated in system. OR error shown if data is invalid.

1. User initiates edit. 2. System loads existing user data, displays edit form. 3. User modifies data. 4. System validates correctness. If invalid → error, allow retry. 5. System saves updated data, shows success message.
UC-06: Manage User Accounts — ADMIN ONLY (Valdyti naudotojų paskyras)
Allow administrator to view, search, and delete user accounts.
Administrator is authenticated with admin role.
User list viewed, specific user viewed, or user deleted with confirmation. System shows appropriate messages.

1. Admin initiates account management. 2. System shows all users list. 3. Admin may search by criteria → system shows filtered results or 'no results' message. 4. Admin selects a user. 5. Admin chooses action: (a) View — system shows user info; (b) Delete — system requests confirmation. Admin confirms → system deletes user + shows success. Admin cancels → system shows cancellation message. 6. Admin continues with other users or ends process.
UC-07: Manage Garden User Access (Valdyti daržo naudotojų prieigas)
Allow a garden owner to control which users have access to their plot.
Garden owner is authenticated and has permission to manage plot access.
User access rights are added, updated, or removed for the plot.

1. Owner initiates access management. 2. System shows list of current users and their access levels. 3. Owner selects action: (a) Edit/remove existing access — selects user; if remove: system removes access; if change level: system shows role selector, owner picks, system updates. (b) Add new user — owner enters user data, system shows role selector, owner picks level, system saves new access rights.
UC-08: Manage Plot Plan (Valdyti sklypo planą)
Create, view, edit, delete, and export plot plans.
Plot plan created/updated/viewed/deleted. User can additionally export to PDF.

Owner selects action: (A) CREATE: system shows data input form → user enters data → system validates → if invalid: error + retry → if valid: system generates empty plot template → user marks plot boundaries on map → system validates & saves boundaries → user manages plant zones (add/edit/delete zones; each with data form, validation, save) → user manages plants within zones (add from available list, edit, delete) → system saves all changes. (B) VIEW: system shows existing plans list → user selects → system renders plan. (C) DELETE: system requests confirmation → user confirms → system deletes. (D) EXPORT to PDF: see UC-09.
UC-09: Export Plan to PDF (Eksportuoti planą į PDF)
Generate a PDF document of the selected plot plan for download.
Garden owner is authenticated and has at least one plot plan.
Garden owner selects 'Export Plan to PDF'.
PDF file of the plot plan is generated and downloaded to user's device.

1. Owner initiates export. 2. System accepts request. 3. System gathers data from plot, zones, and plants. 4. System transforms data into structured report. 5. System generates PDF document. 6. System presents PDF file for download. 7. System shows success confirmation message.
UC-10: Share Plot Data (Dalintis sklypo duomenimis)
Allow a garden owner to share their plot plan with other users.
Owner is authenticated and has at least one plot plan.
Plot sharing settings updated. OR sharing cancelled.

1. Owner initiates sharing. 2. System shows list of owner's plots. 3. Owner selects a plan. 4. System shows sharing settings form. 5. Owner specifies sharing parameters (with whom, sharing method). 6. Owner confirms → system saves settings + shows success. OR Owner cancels → system shows cancellation message.
UC-11: View Community Information (Peržiūrėti informaciją bendruomenėje)
Allow a garden owner to browse publicly shared plot plans from other users.
Community plot plan information shown to user. OR main page shown if browsing ends.

1. Owner initiates community browse. 2. System loads all publicly shared plots and shows list. 3. User may select a specific plan. 4. System shows selected plan details. 5. User can browse more plans or finish.
UC-12: Mark Plant Rotation (Pažymėti augalų rotaciją)
Register crop rotation decisions — assign new planting locations for plants in zones.
Owner is authenticated and has at least one plot plan with defined plant zones and plants.
Updated plant rotation info saved in system with new locations. Rotation data available for planning history analysis.

1. Owner initiates rotation marking. 2. System shows existing plot plans. 3. Owner selects plan. 4. System loads plan data, retrieves plant info, checks rotation recommendations (whether any plants were in the same zone in previous seasons). 5. If violations detected → system notifies owner of alternative planting locations. 6. Owner selects a plant and specifies new planting location/zone. 7. System checks zone suitability (against plant requirements) and compatibility with other plants in zone. If unsuitable → warning shown. 8. Repeat steps 6–7 for each plant as needed. 9. Owner confirms all rotation decisions. 10. System validates compatibility, saves updated rotation info, shows confirmation. OR if rejected: system reverts to previous state.
UC-13: View Planning History (Peržiūrėti planavimo istoriją)
Retrieve plot plan version history for review or for system analysis.
Owner is authenticated and has at least one plot plan.
Owner selects 'View Planning History' OR system calls this automatically during analysis.
Selected plan version shown to user. OR if no history: info message shown. OR in analysis context: data passed to analysis process.

(User-initiated): 1. Owner selects plot plan. 2. System retrieves all versions and planning history data. 3. System shows list of plan versions. 4. If no history: info message, end. 5. Owner selects a specific version. 6. System loads and renders that version. 7. Owner may continue viewing other versions or end. (System-initiated): 1. System auto-selects plan by analysis context. 2. System retrieves all plan versions. 3. If history exists: data passed to analysis process. 4. If no data: analysis receives 'no data' notification.
UC-14: Manage Recommendation Calendar (Rekomendacinio veiksmų kalendoriaus valdymas)
Generate a recommended garden maintenance action calendar based on plot data, plant condition, and environmental conditions.
Owner is authenticated and has a plot plan with plants.
Owner selects 'View or Create Recommendation Calendar' for a selected plot plan.
Generated or updated recommendation calendar is saved and presented to user for review and action management.

1. Owner selects calendar function. 2. System checks if calendar already exists for the selected plan. (A) If EXISTS: system loads and presents existing calendar. Owner can: mark tasks as done, mark used materials, cancel planned actions. (B) If DOES NOT EXIST: 3. Owner enters desired planning period. 4. System collects: plot structure data, zones, plants, plant conditions, environmental data (weather forecast via UC-18). 5. System generates recommended action plan including: maintenance tasks, weather-condition-based actions, materials/resources needed. 6. System saves generated calendar and presents to owner. 7. Owner can mark tasks done, register used materials (see UC-15), or cancel tasks.
UC-15: Mark Used Materials (Pažymėti sunaudotas medžiagas)
Register materials/tools consumed during garden maintenance and update their quantities in inventory.
Owner is authenticated and at least one item exists in inventory.
Inventory quantity of selected item updated. Success or error message shown.

1. Owner initiates material marking. 2. System retrieves and shows inventory items list. 3. Owner selects an item. 4. Owner enters consumed quantity. 5. System validates quantity (non-negative, not exceeding available stock). If invalid → error + allow retry. 6. If valid → system updates item quantity in inventory. 7. System shows success message.
UC-16: Identify Plant Condition (Identifikuoti augalo būklę)
Automatically determine and update a plant's current condition based on growth duration, biological parameters, and existing data.
A plant exists in the system with a planting date and assigned biological parameters.
Plant is assigned an updated condition matching its development stage or health status. Saved in system.

1. System retrieves date of checking period and plant's planting date. 2. Calculates growth duration. 3. System retrieves plant's biological parameters from Plant_care dataset. 4. Checks if plant has disease flag set. If YES → set condition = 'diseased', save, end. 5. If NO disease: system evaluates development stage thresholds: — If germination start period not yet passed → 'planted'; — If germination ongoing → 'germinating'; — If growing period → 'growing'; — If flowering period → 'flowering'; — If maturity period → 'mature'. 6. Additionally: if plant is annual AND maturity end passed → condition = 'dried'. If plant is perennial AND dormancy period → condition = 'regenerating'. 7. System saves updated condition.
Plant condition states (enumeration): planted, germinating, growing, flowering, mature, dried, diseased, regenerating
UC-17: Record Plant Condition (Įrašyti augalo būklę)
Allow owner to manually register a plant condition entry, accumulating historical data.
Owner is authenticated and at least one plant exists.
New plant condition record saved. OR error if data invalid.

1. Owner initiates condition recording. 2. System shows plant list. 3. Owner selects plant. 4. Owner may optionally view previous condition history. 5. Owner selects to create new condition record. 6. System shows condition input form. 7. Owner fills in data. 8. System validates. If invalid → error + retry. 9. System saves record, shows success. 10. Owner may continue (enter another record, select another plant) or end.
UC-18: Get Weather Forecasts (Gauti orų prognozes)
Retrieve current weather forecast data from external system and use it for garden planning.
System can access external weather forecast API (Meteo.lt) OR has previously saved weather data.
New weather forecast data saved in system. OR last available data used if fetch fails.

1. Calendar controller initiates weather forecast request via Weather_forecast_boundary. 2. Weather_forecast_boundary calls Meteo.lt API. 3. External system returns data. 4. System validates data correctness. If valid → process and save as new state. Show success to user. If invalid/error → use last saved weather data, show error notification to user.
UC-19: Get Plant Information (Gauti augalų informaciją)
Retrieve plant care data from Perenual external API for use in care recommendations and planning.
System can access Perenual API OR has previously saved plant data.
New plant care data saved. OR last available data used if fetch fails.

1. Controller initiates request via Plant_care_boundary. 2. Plant_care_boundary calls Perenual API. 3. External system returns data. 4. System validates. If valid → process & integrate into plant data structure. Show success. If invalid/error → use last saved data, show error notification.
UC-20: Analyze Garden Results (Analizuoti daržo rezultatus)
Evaluate garden activity results from multiple analytical perspectives.
Owner is authenticated, has at least one plot plan, and has accumulated historical garden data.
Analysis report generated and saved. Presented to user with selected analysis results and overall plot utility indicators.

1. Owner initiates analysis. 2. System shows available plot plans. 3. Owner selects plan. 4. System requests analysis type selection. Owner chooses: (A) Planning decision analysis → system retrieves historical planning data, extracts plant selections per zone per season, checks rotation violations, calculates plan change frequency. (B) Plant condition analysis → system retrieves historical condition records, sorts chronologically, identifies condition changes, finds critical deterioration points, evaluates improvement trends after maintenance actions. (C) Harvest analysis → system retrieves historical harvest records, calculates total harvest quantities, identifies trends, identifies best-yielding plants, compares actual vs planned harvest. 5. After selected analysis branch: system calculates overall plot utility indicators, generates final summary report, presents to owner.
UC-21: View Harvest History (Peržiūrėti derliaus istoriją)
Retrieve harvest history data for a selected plot — for user review or for automated analysis.
Owner is authenticated and at least one plot plan exists.
Owner selects 'View Harvest History' OR system calls automatically during analysis.
Harvest history data shown to user OR passed to analysis process. If no data: info message shown.

(User-initiated): 1. System shows plot plans list. 2. Owner selects plan. 3. System checks if harvest records exist. If none → info message + option to select another plan. If records exist → system collects and processes data (harvest quantities per plant, harvest dates, time periods). Data presented to owner. (System-initiated): plot selected by analysis context; data passed to analysis; if no records: 'no data' passed.
UC-22: View Plant Condition History (Peržiūrėti augalo būklės istoriją)
Retrieve condition history for a selected plant — for user review or automated analysis.
Owner is authenticated and at least one plant exists.
Owner selects 'View Plant Condition History' OR system calls automatically.
Plant condition history shown to user OR passed to analysis. If no records: info message.

(User-initiated): 1. System shows plant list. 2. Owner selects plant. 3. System retrieves all condition records for plant. 4. If none → info message. 5. If records exist → system forms condition history, presents to owner. (System-initiated): plant(s) selected by context, data passed to analysis.
Create, view, search, edit, and delete plants in the system.
Plant data created/viewed/edited/deleted. Appropriate messages shown.

1. System shows plants list. 2. Owner may search by criteria. 3. Owner selects action: (A) CREATE: input form → validate → if invalid: error; if valid: save, success. (B) VIEW: show plant info. (C) DELETE: confirmation → confirm: delete + success; cancel: cancellation message. (D) EDIT: edit form with existing data → validate → save, success.
Allow owner to register the harvested quantity for a specific plant.
Owner is authenticated and at least one plant exists.
New harvest record created with specified quantity.

1. Owner initiates harvest registration. 2. System shows plant list. 3. Owner selects plant. 4. System requests harvest quantity input. 5. Owner enters quantity. 6. System saves as new harvest record.
UC-25: View Tools & Materials List (Peržiūrėti įrankių ir medžiagų sąrašą)
Allow owner to view and manage tools and materials in inventory.
1. System shows inventory list. 2. Owner may: ADD new items (form → save), EDIT existing items (edit form → save), DELETE items (confirmation → delete). 3. Owner continues or finishes.
Add new tools or materials to inventory.
New inventory record created. OR error if data invalid.
1. System shows input form. 2. Owner enters tool/material data. 3. System validates. If invalid → error + retry. 4. If valid → system saves, shows success.
Owner is authenticated and at least one inventory record exists.
Inventory record updated. OR error if data invalid.

1. System shows selection of inventory records. 2. Owner selects item. 3. System shows edit form with existing data. 4. Owner enters new/corrected data. 5. System validates. If invalid → error + retry. 6. If valid → save, success.
UC-28: Delete Materials & Tools (Šalinti medžiagas ir įrankius)
Owner is authenticated and at least one inventory record exists.
1. System shows selection. 2. Owner selects item. 3. System shows deletion confirmation. 4. Owner confirms → system deletes, shows success. Owner rejects → system shows cancellation message.
Data entered in system must be reliably stored in the database.
After data entry and page reload, all records persist.
Enter test data, reload page, verify data remains.
System pages must load within a short time.
Measure page load time using browser developer tools.
UI must be adapted for different screen sizes (responsive).
All elements render correctly at different resolutions.
Test on desktop and mobile device screens.
Only authenticated users can access system functions.
System pages and functions inaccessible without login.
Attempt to access system functions without being logged in.
System must show clear feedback after successful action.
After data save/update, an informational message is displayed.
Perform data entry test and verify message appears.
System must validate all input data.
Invalid or empty data is NOT allowed to be saved.
Enter invalid data and verify system rejects it.
The database is relational (PostgreSQL). All entity relationships use foreign keys. N:M relationships use junction tables. ACID transactions must be supported.
Unique, not null — used for login
Values: viewer, editor (per Access_rights). User-level role: owner or admin
Geometric/boundary data of the plot (map boundaries)
Whether plot is publicly shared in community
Many-to-many junction table between garden owners and plots
Values: clay, peaty, greasy, rocky, sandy
N:1 — a plant belongs to a zone
Values: cereal, forage, fruit, herb, legume, oilseed, vegetable
Whether plant currently has a disease
Whether plant is perennial (true) or annual (false)
External data from Perenual API — cached locally
Skip watering if rain exceeds this threshold
Temperature above which extra watering needed
Wind speed above which protection needed
Plant condition at time of record
Nullable — task may target a specific plant
Nullable — task may target a zone
Values: fertilize, harvest, planting, rest, spray, transplant, watering
City for which forecast is valid
planted — Plant has been placed in soil, no growth yet
germinating — Seed has germinated, sprout visible
flowering — Plant is in flowering stage
mature — Plant has reached full maturity / ready for harvest
dried — Plant has withered (ONLY for annual plants after maturity period ends)
diseased — Plant is sick (set when disease = true; overrides developmental stage)
regenerating — Plant is in dormancy / recovery period (ONLY for perennial plants)
pending — Task is planned but not yet executed
completed — Task has been marked as done
material — Consumable material (fertilizer, pesticide, soil, etc.)
tool — Reusable tool (shovel, watering can, etc.)
viewer — Can view plot but not modify
editor — Can view and modify plot
Fetch current and forecast weather data for use in action calendar generation
If API unavailable or returns invalid data → use last saved weather_forecast records
Checks: extreme weather flag, rain skip threshold (mm), frost threshold (°C), heat extra water threshold (°C), wind protection threshold (km/h)
Fetch plant care parameters for use in condition identification and calendar recommendation
If API unavailable → use last saved plant_care records

description, conditions, growing/germinating/mature/flowering/regenerating durations, watering/fertilizing/pest_check intervals, rain_skip_threshold_mm, frost_temp_threshold_c, heat_extra_water_temp_c, wind_protection_kmh
Send password reset code to user's email
User initiates 'Forgot Password' and email found in DB
System generates reset code → stores in users.reset_code → calls send_reset_email() → email server delivers message
The system uses a three-layer MVC architecture:
UI Layer (Frontend — React): All application windows/pages organized by functional area. Handles user interaction, displays data, sends requests to controller layer.
Controller Layer (Backend — Laravel): Handles all business logic, request processing, validation, coordination between UI and data layers. Organized by the same functional sub-systems.
Data Layer (PostgreSQL via Laravel Eloquent ORM): All domain entities and their relationships as defined in Section 5.
Registration, login, logout, password reset, profile edit
Admin user account listing, searching, deletion
Plot CRUD, zone management, plant placement, sharing, PDF export, history, analysis, rotation, harvest
Recommendation calendar generation, task management, weather integration
Tools/materials CRUD, quantity tracking, consumption marking
Plant CRUD, condition recording, condition history
Community post creation, public plot browsing
Meteo.lt weather API, Perenual plant API, Email server
Main Page → Plot List | Action Calendar | Inventory | Community | Analytics | [Admin: User Management]
Plot List → Plot Detail View → Plant List | Plant Detail | Rotation Planning
Analytics → Summary data, garden result analysis
Community → Shared plot list → Individual plot view | Create post
Admin → User list → User detail | Delete user
A user can own zero or more plots. A plot can have multiple owners (N:M via access_rights).
A plot has one or more plant zones. Each zone belongs to exactly one plot (1:N).
A plant zone can contain zero or more plants. Each plant belongs to one zone.
One plot can have multiple task calendars (one per planning period).
A task calendar has zero or more tasks.
A plant can have zero or more condition history records.
A plant zone has rotation history records tracking which plants were grown when.
Each garden owner has their own inventory (not shared between owners).
Community posts can reference a plot. A plot can be marked as shareable (share = true).
Password reset: code generated, stored in reset_code, sent by email, verified, then used once and deleted.
Plant condition is both manually recordable (UC-17) AND automatically computed by the system (UC-16).
Disease overrides developmental stage: if disease=true → condition='diseased' regardless of growth stage.
Annual plants (reusable=false): when growth ends → condition='dried'.
Perennial plants (reusable=true): when dormancy period → condition='regenerating'.
Calendar generation requires: plot data + plant conditions + weather forecast. Generates task list per date.
Rotation check: system warns if a plant was in the same zone in a previous season (uses rotation_history).
PDF export collects plot + zone + plant data and generates downloadable document.
This section maps the controller and boundary classes required per sub-system.
Password_reset_controller: generate_reset_code(), start_reset_code_generation(), request_reset(), verify_code(), submit_new_password(), validate_password()
Profile_router: navigates to profile edit scenarios
Account_controller: get_all_users(), search_users(), get_user(), delete_user(), confirm_delete(), reject_delete()

Task_calendar_controller: get_tasks_for_date(), validate_forecast(), process_forecast(), validate_plant_care(), process_plant_care(), calculate_growing_time(), calculate_days(), check_if_any_tasks_needed(), check_if_weather_is_extreme(), check_if_last_plant(), check_if_last_day()
Calendar_router, Task_router: navigation between calendar scenarios
Plot_controller: create(), update(), delete(), validate(), get plot data operations
Rotation_controller: check rotation rules, assign plants to zones, enforce agronomic principles
Analyze_controller: harvest analysis, plant layout analysis, utility indicators
History_controller: save and retrieve plot plan version history
Harvest_controller: register and analyze harvest data
Share_controller: configure access rights, share plot data
Export_controller: generate PDF export of plot plan
Plot_router: navigation between all plot management scenarios
Plant_controller: get_all_plants(), get_plants(), validate_data(), get_plant(), delete()

Inventory_controller: prepare_delete(), delete_item(), select(), prepare_edit(), validate_edit(), validate(), prepare_add(), validate_addition(), open_inventory(), select_action(), update_amount(), validate_amount(), reject_delete()
Community_controller: get_shared_plot(), get_community_posts(), create_post(), delete_post(), validate_post(), get_public_posts()
Email + password form. 'Forgot password' link. Submit button.
Registration form with email, password, name, surname. Validation.
Email input for reset code request.
Dashboard: user role display, plot count, collaboration status. Quick-access cards for plots. Navigation to all modules.
View and edit user profile data (name, surname, etc).
List of user's plots as cards. Create new plot button.
Visual map-based editor. Mark plot boundaries. Zone management (add/edit/delete). Plant management within zones.
Two-pane layout: calendar on left (date selector), task list on right for selected date. Task type, related plant, materials, status shown. Mark done / cancel actions.
List of existing calendars per plot. Create calendar option.
Input form for planning period (start date, end date).
Analysis type selector (planning/condition/harvest). Results display with charts/summaries.
List of all tools/materials with quantities. Add/edit/delete buttons.
Forms for CRUD operations on inventory items.
Grid of publicly shared plots from all users.
Detailed view of a specific shared plot.
Form to create community post (select plot, enter content).
List of plants with search/filter. Create plant button.
Plant detail / create / edit / delete pages
Form to record new condition entry for a plant.
Timeline/list of past condition records for a plant.
Visual zone-based interface showing rotation recommendations, warnings, and confirmation.
Full user list with search. Select user for view/delete.
The calendar generation (UC-14) uses the following logic:
INPUTS: Plot ID, planning period (start_date → end_date), plant list with conditions, weather forecast data, plant_care data, inventory items
FOR EACH DATE in planning period:
Fetch weather forecast for date. If weather is extreme (see thresholds) → generate weather-warning tasks.
Call Identify Plant Condition (UC-16) → determine current condition_type.
Retrieve plant_care record for this plant type.
Check if any tasks are needed: evaluate watering_interval_days, fertilizing_interval_days, pest_check_interval_days.

Check weather conditions: if precipitation > rain_skip_threshold_mm → skip watering. If temperature < frost_temp_threshold_c → add frost protection task. If temperature > heat_extra_water_temp_c → add extra watering task. If wind > wind_protection_kmh → add wind protection task.
Generate Task entries with appropriate task_type, name, related plant/zone, and item requirements.
Check inventory: for each generated task needing materials, call check_if_enough() on Inventory_item. If insufficient → flag the task with needed materials info.
SAVE: Create task_calendar record, save all Task records linked to it. Associate weather_forecast records.
Backend must use Laravel's Eloquent ORM for all database operations. No raw SQL unless absolutely necessary for complex analytics queries.
All relationships must be enforced via Eloquent relationships AND PostgreSQL foreign key constraints.
Use Laravel migrations for all schema changes. No manual DB alterations.
Use Laravel's built-in authentication (Sanctum or session-based) for user auth.
All forms must use Laravel server-side validation. Frontend validation is additional, not a replacement.
React frontend communicates with Laravel backend via REST API endpoints.
The plot geometry field uses JSON to store map boundary coordinates.
PDF export must include: plot name, all zones with names, all plants per zone with names and conditions.
Community: only plots with share=true are visible in community browsing.
Access control: check role in access_rights for every plot operation. Editor can modify. Viewer can only read.
Admin role is set in users.role field. Separate from plot-level access_rights.
Rotation check must query rotation_history for the same plant_zone_id and overlapping time periods.
Planning history: each time a plot is significantly modified, save a version snapshot for history.
Plant condition is auto-identified server-side — no user action needed for UC-16. It is triggered from calendar generation and task processing.
Weather data: fallback to most recent weather_forecast record if API call fails.
All monetary/quantity fields use DOUBLE PRECISION in PostgreSQL.
System must be accessible on both desktop browsers and mobile browsers (responsive CSS).
Page load time target: ≤ 3 seconds for all primary pages.
